fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Ensuring that side access is clearly marked and any obstructions are minimized could improve emergency responder access and movement around the property. Consider enhancing visibility by utilizing adequate lighting and reflective markers to guide personnel during critical operations, particularly in low-light conditions. Additionally, maintaining the landscaped area in a manner that prevents encroachment into access zones may enhance maneuverability for emergency vehicles and personnel. These actions could facilitate more efficient navigation and effective staging, ultimately supporting the execution of emergency operations on the property.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could be achieved by increasing separation between vegetation and the structure, particularly focusing on areas where dense canopies and scattered trees are in close proximity. This may involve thinning vegetation immediately adjacent to the residence and managing the density of the tree canopy to limit fuel continuity. Establishing and preserving open areas between the structure and surrounding vegetation can further reduce potential fire exposure. Managing the vegetated perimeter by reducing concentrations of combustible materials can improve access and visibility for firefighters, thus supporting suppression efforts. These actions, combined with ongoing maintenance of defensible space and improved fuel breaks, could enhance long-term wildfire resilience and preparedness for the property.
